context: Recent composite feed (Aave-on-Mantle TVL, USD 8-dec): 16000000000000000, 15600000000000000, 15900000000000000, 12200000000000000, 12500000000000000. A large step-down between snapshots 3 and 4 (~$37M outflow) then stabilization near $124M. News: a competing Mantle lending protocol launched higher deposit yields 24h ago; some capital rotated out of Aave.
reasoning: Observed: a regime break — TVL was ~$159M, dropped sharply to ~$122M on a single day, then steadied near $124M. The drop aligns with a competitor's higher-yield launch pulling rate-sensitive capital. Hypothesis: the bulk of the rotation is done (the last two points stabilized), so the new level near $123–125M is the anchor, not the pre-drop $159M. I center slightly above the latest value to allow partial mean-reversion as Aave rates re-equilibrate, but I do NOT anchor to the old level — that's the classic mistake after a structural shift. Band covers a modest re-stabilization range. Confidence ~63%: stabilization signal is fairly clear, but post-shock flows can stay choppy for another day.
summary: Deposits in Aave on Mantle dropped to about $124M after a competitor offered better rates, and I expect them to stay near that new level — roughly $118M to $132M.
confidence_rationale: I centered on the new lower level instead of the old one, with a moderate range since money can still shift around after such a move.
